site.name: Shovon Mandal
site.title: Lecturer, Department of Computer Science and Engineering
site.affiliation: Northern University of Business and Technology Khulna, Bangladesh
site.aspiration: Aspiring to pursue advanced graduate study (M.Sc./Ph.D.) in secure distributed systems, blockchain security, and NFC/IoT transaction security while contributing to rigorous research, teaching support, technical implementation, and scholarly collaboration.
site.headline: Blockchain-Based Secure Digital Payments · NFC/IoT Transaction Security · Secure Distributed Systems · Research-Oriented Computing

about: I am a Computer Science and Engineering lecturer and graduate researcher with academic interests in blockchain-based secure digital payments, NFC-enabled transaction systems, secure distributed architectures.

My research background integrates theoretical interest and implementation-oriented practice. Through my M.Sc. and B.Sc. thesis work, publications, and prototype development, I have explored practical approaches to secure payment infrastructures, decentralized systems, and transaction verification models.

Beyond research, I bring classroom, laboratory, tutoring, and mentoring experience. I am comfortable working under academic pressure, adapting to new research environments, and learning new technologies quickly when a project or research problem requires it.

highlightCards.title: Graduate Study Direction
highlightCards.text: Interested in advanced graduate study and long-term research in secure distributed systems, blockchain security, FinTech security, and IoT/NFC transaction security.

highlightCards.title: Academic Readiness
highlightCards.text: Prepared to contribute through literature review, technical documentation, prototype development, teaching support, student mentoring, and collaborative research execution.

highlightCards.title: Research Disposition
highlightCards.text: Adaptable, deadline-aware, and motivated to work in rigorous academic environments with a strong emphasis on learning, contribution, and publication.

projects.title: Blockchain and NFC-Based Secure Cashless Payment Prototype
projects.description: A secure cashless payment prototype integrating blockchain-based transaction verification with NFC-enabled payment interaction.
highlights: Implemented Arduino and Python modules for NFC-based transaction processing.
Supported experimental validation of secure cashless payment workflows.
Connected implementation evidence with the ICKECS 2024 publication.

projects.title: IOTA-Based Payment Website and Decentralized Transaction Demonstration
projects.description: A web-based prototype demonstrating IOTA-supported payment interaction and decentralized transaction flow.
highlights: Developed front-end and backend components for payment demonstration and user interaction.
Linked implementation work to decentralized transaction and node-confirmation research.
Connected conceptually with the ICCIT 2024 publication.

additionalProjects.title: Student Management Android Application

additionalProjects.title: Traffic Light System
additionalProjects.description: Hardware-oriented project demonstrating control logic, digital system behavior, and implementation concepts.

additionalProjects.title: Fire Alarm Integrated with Mobile SIM
additionalProjects.description: A hardware project combining alert functionality with mobile SIM-based communication and notification concepts.

additionalProjects.title: PHP-Laravel and E-Commerce Web Projects
additionalProjects.description: Web development projects involving PHP, Laravel, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and database-driven functionality.

strengths: Comfortable working under academic pressure in research, teaching, and project-driven environments.
Adaptive to new research settings and able to learn unfamiliar technologies quickly when a research problem requires it.
Prepared for literature review, technical writing, implementation support, teaching support, and scholarly collaboration.
Interested in building a sustained graduate research trajectory through rigorous study, implementation, and publication.
